name: Detour Drummond Community
description_short: DeTour Drummond Community Credit Union is a member-owned financial cooperative offering loans, share accounts, and branch/ATM access to residents in Michigan's Upper Peninsula.
description_long:
DeTour Drummond Community Credit Union (DDCCU) is a not-for-profit, member-owned credit union serving the DeTour area and surrounding communities in Michigan's Upper Peninsula. As a credit union, it operates under NCUA federal insurance and operates with a governance structure centered on member benefits rather than shareholder profits. The institution is staffed by a small, local team led by CEO Hilary Fountain and includes dedicated loan officers and member service representatives who provide personalized service to account holders.

DDCCU offers core credit union services including loan applications, share accounts (savings products), share certificates (CD equivalents), mobile banking, and access to extended branch and ATM networks through shared branching and CO-OP ATM systems. Members can apply for loans directly through the institution and access their accounts remotely via mobile platforms. The credit union provides standard financial services typical of community-based credit unions, with a focus on local relationship banking.

What distinguishes DDCCU is its deep community integration in a rural Michigan location, with a hands-on management team and direct access to loan officers like Sheila Seaman (Senior Loan Officer) and Marty Kadolph. The small staff size suggests personalized member service rather than automated processing. Membership in shared branching and CO-OP ATM networks extends member convenience beyond the single local branch.

For consumers seeking traditional credit union services in the Upper Peninsula, DDCCU provides standard offerings. The website provides limited detail on loan products, rates, terms, or membership eligibility requirements. Prospective members should contact the institution directly to confirm product availability, pricing, and whether they meet membership criteria. As a small community credit union, service capabilities and product range may be more limited than larger regional or national credit unions.
pros: Member-owned not-for-profit structure returns profits to members rather than shareholders
NCUA federal insurance protection on accounts (standard credit union protection)
Access to CO-OP ATM network and shared branching system beyond single physical location
Personalized service from named, dedicated loan officers (Sheila Seaman, Marty Kadolph)
Mobile banking available for remote account access
Direct CEO/management accessibility typical of small community institutions
Local presence and community integration in DeTour service area
cons: Limited online information about specific loan products, rates, APRs, or terms
Small staff may mean limited hours or service availability compared to larger institutions
No online account opening or loan application details provided on website
Single physical branch location limits in-person service options
No information about membership eligibility or joining requirements on website
best_for: DeTour area residents seeking relationship banking from a local, member-owned institution
Consumers who prefer credit unions over traditional banks and value community connection
Members seeking basic savings accounts, CDs, and personal loans from a trusted local lender
Upper Peninsula residents wanting access to extended ATM and branch networks via CO-OP system
diagnosis: DeTour Drummond Community Credit Union is best for Upper Peninsula residents and members seeking traditional credit union services from a local, member-owned institution. The main caveat is that the website provides minimal detail on specific products, rates, terms, or membership requirements—prospective members must contact the credit union directly at (906) 493-5263 to understand available loans, share products, and eligibility criteria.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.

meta_description: Detour Drummond Community — NCUA-insured credit union in DRUMMOND ISLAND, MI. Member-owned, not-for-profit. Loans, savings, and member services.
